name: Río de Belelle – Belelle Waterfall trail
name_original: Río de Belelle – sendero de Fervenza de Belelle
region_intro: Hiking around Neda offers a diverse landscape characterized by river valleys, waterfalls, and sections of historical pilgrimage routes. The region features a mix of forested paths, open countryside, and trails alongside the Belelle River. Hikers can expect varied terrain, from gentle riverside walks to more undulating sections with moderate elevation changes. Best hiking trails around Neda The most popular hiking route is Río de Belelle – Belelle Waterfall trail, a 7.5 miles (12.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 26 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the Belelle River, leading to its notable waterfall. Another top favourite among local hikers is Belelle Waterfall – Belelle River Waterfall loop from Neda, a moderate 9.3 miles (14.9 km) path. This loop offers extensive views of the river valley and surrounding woodlands. Local hikers also love the Camino Inglés in Narón – Magnolia of Xuvia loop from Neda, a 2.9 miles (4.7 km) trail leading through a mix of urban and natural landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 13 minutes. Hiking around Neda is defined by river paths, forested sections, and historical routes like the Camino Inglés. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to moderate excursions. The routes in Neda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 250 reviews. More than 1500 hikers have used komoot to explore Neda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Neda?
A: Neda offers a diverse network of 12 hiking trails, catering to various preferences. You'll find a good mix, with 5 easy routes perfect for a relaxed stroll and 7 moderate trails for those seeking a bit more challenge.

Q: What is the overall difficulty level of hiking in Neda?
A: The hiking trails in Neda are primarily easy to moderate. There are 5 easy routes, ideal for beginners or a leisurely day out, and 7 moderate routes that offer a bit more challenge with varied terrain and elevation changes. There are no difficult trails listed in the immediate area.

Q: Are there any hikes that feature waterfalls in Neda?
A: Yes, Neda is known for its beautiful waterfalls. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991367929/rio-de-belelle-belelle-waterfall-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río de Belelle – Belelle Waterfall trail&lt;/a&gt;, which leads directly to the notable Belelle Waterfall. You can also explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/985047" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belelle River Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2725985" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belelle Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; itself, which are prominent natural features in the region.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Neda?
A: Absolutely. Neda has 5 easy routes that are well-suited for families or those looking for a less strenuous walk. These trails often follow river paths and offer pleasant scenery without significant elevation gains. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31892559" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belelle Waterfall – Belelle River Waterfall loop from A Fervenza&lt;/a&gt; is an easy option.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Neda?
A: Yes, several routes in Neda are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A great example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3990401" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belelle Waterfall – Belelle River Waterfall loop from Neda&lt;/a&gt;, which provides extensive views of the river valley and woodlands.

Q: What natural landmarks can I see while hiking in Neda?
A: Beyond the waterfalls, Neda's trails offer access to several natural landmarks. You can visit the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6599577" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Magnolia of Xuvia&lt;/a&gt;, explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7546440" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural das Fragas do Eume&lt;/a&gt;, or ascend to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2890835" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte da Vela Summit&lt;/a&gt; for panoramic views.

Q: Are there any historical sites or points of interest near the hiking trails?
A: Yes, the Neda area is rich in history. You can encounter historical sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/936577" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Saint John of Caaveiro&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3546198" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of San Martiño de Xuvia&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7469393" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parish Church of St James in Pontedeume&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5770278" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pontedeume Bridge&lt;/a&gt; is also a notable historical structure.

Q: When is the best time of year to go hiking in Neda?
A: For the best experience, especially if you're interested in river trekking or enjoying the waterfalls, the period from May to September is ideal. During these months, the weather is warm, making it perfect for exploring the Neda Gorge and its natural pools. However, the region's diverse trails can be enjoyed year-round, with different seasons offering unique scenery.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about the trails in Neda?
A: The trails in Neda are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 250 reviews. Hikers often praise the beautiful river paths, the tranquility of the forested sections, and the well-maintained routes that offer a mix of natural beauty and historical context, including parts of the Camino Inglés.

Q: Are there any trails that are part of the Camino Inglés in Neda?
A: Yes, Neda's hiking network includes sections of the historic Camino Inglés. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24638457" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Inglés in Narón – Magnolia of Xuvia loop from Neda&lt;/a&gt; offers a route that combines urban and natural landscapes, allowing you to experience a part of this pilgrimage path.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Neda's hiking trails?
A: Neda's trails feature varied terrain, characterized by river valleys, lush forests, and open countryside. You'll encounter paths alongside the Belelle River, some gentle and others with moderate elevation changes. The region is known for its impressive gorge formations and rich vegetation, providing a diverse hiking experience.
tips: A spectacular waterfall on the Belelle River. The path that reaches the waterfall is easy to follow for those who are minimally trained in hiking and is fully signposted.

The Belelle River is part of the Fragas do Eume Natural Park, running northeast to the Ferrol estuary. On its way, the river crosses rough terrain, creating pools and waterfalls. The power of the river was used to create sails for the Spanish navy. Many water channels that diverted river water to industry still remain today
